creator: swhetzel@gmail.com
text:
>Bablefish translation:
>
>when attempt to install FreeBSD Release 1 in my amd turion 64 the
process is >interrupted practicamente at the beginning, they begin to
appear in screen in >cascade form the contents of the registries eax,
etc... time and time again, so >quickly that its content cannot nor be
read.

This is the same problem as reported in PR 105514 when booting from
the install CDs:

   http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=105514

To get past this problem you need to hold down the space bar as the
system boots, then when you see /boot/kernel/kernel on the screen, you
can release the space bar.  The system will then boot into the
sysinstall, and you can then install the OS.  Once the OS is
installed, the system will boot normally.

Scot
